    Get In Touch

    Tokenomic provides concise, tailored courses, consultations, and market analysis for DeFi integration. Our experts span technical, legal, financial, and artistic fields, delivering comprehensive support. Through workshops and insights, we enable institutions to succeed in digital finance.

    Terms of Service

    Terms of Service

    • Home
    • Terms of Service

    Terms of Service for Tokenomic.org

    Effective Date: April 2, 2026 | Last Updated: April 2, 2026

    Welcome to Tokenomic.org (the "Platform"). These Terms of Service ("Terms") govern your access to and use of our decentralized educational infrastructure, marketplace, and smart contract integrations.

    By accessing the Platform, connecting a Web3 wallet, or interacting with our smart contracts, you agree to be bound by these Terms. If you do not agree, do not use the Platform.

    1. Nature of the Platform

    Tokenomic.org is a decentralized, community-led infrastructure protocol. We provide a digital marketplace that connects institutional learners with independent educators, consultants, and researchers (collectively, "Contributors").

    • No Fiduciary Duty: We are a technology provider, not an educational institution, broker, or financial advisor.
    • Peer-to-Peer: All engagements (courses, advisory, audits) are direct agreements between the Learner and the Contributor.

    2. Not Financial Advice

    ALL CONTENT ON TOKENOMIC.ORG IS FOR EDUCATIONAL AND ARCHITECTURAL PURPOSES ONLY.

    Nothing contained in our modules, research papers, modeling labs, or consultant advisory sessions constitutes financial, investment, legal, or tax advice. DeFi protocols carry extreme risk. You are solely responsible for your own economic models, protocol designs, and institutional investments.

    3. Web3 Wallet & Smart Contract Interactions

    To access premium modules or offer services, you must use a non-custodial Web3 wallet (e.g., MetaMask, Rabby, Institutional Custodians).

    • Self-Custody: You are solely responsible for maintaining the security of your private keys and seed phrases. Tokenomic.org cannot recover lost wallets.
    • Smart Contract Risk: Transactions on the Platform utilize decentralized protocols (e.g., Helio, 0xSplits). While we require audits for integrated contracts, you acknowledge the inherent risks of blockchain technology, including bugs, hacks, and network congestion.
    • Immutability: Blockchain transactions are irreversible. Once a transaction is confirmed on-chain, it cannot be undone by Tokenomic.org.

    4. Payments, Fees, and Revenue Splits

    All pricing on the Platform is denominated in stablecoins (e.g., USDC) or native network tokens.

    • Automated Splits: Payments for courses and services are routed through decentralized split contracts. By default, revenue is distributed as follows: 90% to the Contributor, 5% to the Tokenomic Treasury, and 5% to Community Rewards.
    • Gas Fees: You are responsible for paying all network transaction fees ("gas") associated with your interactions.
    • Refund Policy: Because payments are settled instantly and irreversibly via smart contracts to independent Contributors, Tokenomic.org cannot issue refunds. Any dispute regarding service quality must be resolved directly with the Contributor.

    5. Contributor Obligations (Educators & Consultants)

    If you list a course, research paper, or advisory service on the Platform:

    • Accuracy: You guarantee that the expertise and modeling data you provide is accurate and original.
    • Compliance: You are responsible for ensuring your offerings comply with the regulations of your local jurisdiction.
    • Content Ownership: You retain the intellectual property rights to your educational materials, granting Tokenomic.org a decentralized, perpetual license to display them via IPFS/Arweave or our frontend interface.

    6. Institutional Compliance (KYB / AML)

    While Tokenomic.org is a permissionless protocol, direct 1-on-1 institutional advisory services offered by Contributors may require Know Your Business (KYB) or Anti-Money Laundering (AML) checks.

    Learners and Contributors are solely responsible for conducting any required compliance checks off-platform before engaging in specialized, high-tier advisory services.

    7. Intellectual Property & On-Chain Credentials

    • Platform IP: The Tokenomic.org name, logos, UI/UX design, and proprietary frontend code are owned by the Tokenomic DAO/Core Team.
    • Badges & Certifications: Completion of modules may result in the issuance of Soulbound Tokens (SBTs) or verifiable credentials. These are non-transferable representations of your educational progress and hold no monetary value.

    8. Limitation of Liability

    To the maximum extent permitted by law, Tokenomic.org, its core contributors, and the Tokenomic DAO shall not be liable for any indirect, incidental, consequential, or punitive damages, including loss of profits, data, or digital assets, arising out of your use of the Platform, reliance on any educational content, or failure of any smart contract.

    9. Governance and Amendments

    Tokenomic.org is governed by its community. These Terms may be updated periodically based on decentralized governance votes. Continued use of the Platform after changes have been enacted on-chain or published on this page constitutes your acceptance of the revised Terms.

    10. Dispute Resolution

    Any disputes arising from these terms will be resolved through decentralized arbitration protocols (e.g., Kleros) as defined by the Tokenomic DAO governance framework, prior to seeking traditional legal remedies.

    Contact Us

    For questions about these Terms of Service, contact us at info@tokenomic.org.

    Community-led DeFi intelligence. Access expert-driven courses, professional advisory, and deep research to architect and scale institutional digital economies.
